name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
description: Agent tools for managing workspace shared files: list, read, write, delete, and organize files.

skill.name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
skill.content:
---
name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
description: Tools to manage workspace shared files
always: true
---

You can manage workspace shared files via the deskclaw_shared_files.py script.

## Usage

```bash
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py <action> [options]
```

## Actions

- `list_files [--path /]` -- List files and directories at a path
- `read_file --file-id ID` -- Read file content (returns base64-encoded content)
- `write_file --file PATH [--filename NAME] [--parent-path /] [--content-type TYPE]` -- Upload a local file (recommended)
- `write_file --content-b64 DATA --filename NAME [--parent-path /] [--content-type TYPE]` -- Upload base64 content (legacy, avoid)
- `copy_file --file-id ID [--target-parent-path /] [--target-filename NAME]` -- Copy a file to another location
- `delete_file --file-id ID` -- Delete a file
- `mkdir --name NAME [--parent-path /]` -- Create a directory
- `get_file_url --file-id ID` -- Get a download URL for a file

## Usage Rules
- **Always use `--file PATH` for uploading**: generate the file locally first, then upload with `--file`
- `--filename` and `--content-type` are auto-inferred from the file path when using `--file`
- Use `copy_file` to duplicate existing shared files instead of downloading and re-uploading
- Use `list_files` to browse directory contents before reading or writing
- Organize files in subdirectories using `mkdir` for better structure
- Shared files are accessible to all workspace members (human and AI)

## Examples

```bash
# List root directory
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py list_files --path /

# Create a subdirectory
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py mkdir --name reports --parent-path /

# Write a file to shared storage (generate locally, then upload)
echo "Report content" > /tmp/report.md
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py write_file --file /tmp/report.md --parent-path /reports

# Read a file
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py read_file --file-id <id>

# Copy a file to another directory
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py copy_file --file-id <id> --target-parent-path /archive

# Delete a file
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py delete_file --file-id <id>

# Get download URL
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py get_file_url --file-id <id>
```
